title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During a dinner of vegetable soup and spaghetti, Hercule Poirot sits with Mrs. Haberdasher and Celia Austin. Celia, a pharmacist at St. Catherine's Hospital, clarifies that she is a resident rather than a student. She provides Poirot with a list of the other residents, including Indian students Chandra Lal and Gopal Ram, Dutch student Miss Reinecke, and the Egyptian political fanatic Ahmed Ali. She also identifies several students: Nigel Chapman, Patricia Lane, Lenn Bateson, Valerie Hobhouse, Colin McNab, Sally Finch, Genevieve Marycourt, Rene Harlow, Jean Thompson, Archibald Booth, and Elizabeth Johnston. Celia notes that Nigel tends to stir up emotions, Lenn is easily irritated, and Colin is light-hearted; Poirot observes that Celia appears to be in love with Colin, though Colin ignores her.

During the meal, Nigel Chapman discusses crime as a form of creative art and suggests police officers have sadistic tendencies, a comment defended by an anxious Patricia Lane. Celia mentions that she previously danced with Nigel during May Week at Cambridge University.

After dinner, the group moves to the public lounge for coffee, where Poirot delivers a forty-five-minute speech. He uses a story about a soap manufacturer in Liège who poisoned his wife to illustrate the importance of preventing murder. Following the speech, Colin McNab challenges Poirot, asking why he is really there. Poirot admits that his hostess confided troubling matters to him. This admission causes Lancelot Bates to react with anger, Nigel to smirk, and Celia to react with shock, claiming she was right.
